data.title: Romania's economy stalls in Q1, Poland surprises on upside
data.description:
Romania's economy stalled in the first quarter amid an extended political crisis and worries about a potential downturn, underperforming most peers in central and eastern Europe.

In Poland, preliminary data showed the economy grew more than 3% year-on-year for a second consecutive quarter, while Slovakia's economic growth continued, but at a slowing pace.

Romania's GDP grew 0.2% year-on-year in the first quarter, well below a Reuters poll forecast of 1.2%, statistics office data showed. The economy stagnated in quarterly terms.

Romania, the region's second biggest economy, has been in turmoil since hard-right presidential front-runner George Simion's surge in a re-run first-round vote led to the collapse of the pro-Western coalition government.

Worries about Romania's ability to rein in the EU's highest budget deficit and stave off a credit rating downgrade are high before a run-off vote on May 18.

The breakdown of GDP data will be released in June.

"We suspect a slowdown in consumer spending, potentially more significant than anticipated. Investment growth may also have been weaker than expected," Erste Group Bank said, adding concerns about potential tax changes under the next government could cause growth revisions.

Analysts in a Reuters poll forecast the economy will grow 1.6% this year, less than the 2.5% estimate the government used when drafting the 2025 budget. The country's largest employer association, Concordia, told Reuters tariffs could reduce economic output by between 0.3% and 0.7%.

Economists and ratings agencies have said that the government which will emerge once a new president nominates a prime minister will almost certainly need to raise taxes to reduce Romania's budget deficit, which would also hamper growth.

CONSUMPTION DRIVING GROWTH

Central and eastern Europe's economies have leaned on rebounding consumer activity to drive growth now that the inflation surges of recent years have abated.

In Poland, the statistics office data showed first-quarter gross domestic product grew by 3.2% year-on-year, above a 3.1% forecast. Quarter-on-quarter growth reached 0.7%.

"I would assume that consumption is continuing to show some moderate growth and that investments are gradually starting to emerge from this trough," Piotr Bielski, head of economic analysis at Santander Bank Polska, said.

Poland's budget anticipates growth of about 4% this year to help it lower the country's deficit, which has ballooned to double the European Union-mandated 3% of GDP limit.

Poland's Finance Minister Andrzej Domanski said on X that growing investments would help growth.

Separate data showed April's inflation reading at 4.3%, a touch higher than an earlier 4.2% estimate, which combined with growth data may warrant some caution from Poland's central bank in easing monetary policy.

Slovakia's economy grew by 0.9% year-on-year, below 1% for the first time in two years, the statistics office said. Household consumption remained a main growth driver.

Thursday's preliminary first-quarter GDP releases come after the Czech Republic reported a 2.0% year-on-year rise in the first quarter while Hungary reported stagnation in year-on-year terms and a quarter-on-quarter decline.

data.title: NetEase's Profit, Revenue Jump in Q1
data.description:
NetEase recorded a rise in attributable net income in the first quarter to 10.3 billion yuan from 7.63 billion yuan a year prior, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Earnings per share rose to 3.21 yuan in the three months from 2.35 yuan in the same period last year.

The online game developer's net revenue rose 7.4% to 28.8 billion yuan from 26.9 billion yuan in the year-ago period.

The board approved a dividend of $0.135 per share, or $0.6750 per ADS, for the first quarter, payable to shareholders of record on May 30.

data.title: Agnico Eagle Invests Additional $90 Million in Foran Mining
data.description:
Agnico Eagle Mines late on Wednesday said it has agreed to subscribe for 30 million voting common shares of Foran Mining for $90 million.

The non-brokered private placement is expected to close in two tranches, subject to closing conditions including approval of the TSX and of Foran shareholders for the second tranche. Closing of the first tranche is scheduled for May 28, and the second tranche is expected to close as soon as practicable following receipt of shareholder approval.

On closing of the first tranche, Agnico Eagle is expected to own roughly 64.5 million common shares, representing 13.1% of the issued and outstanding shares of Foran on an undiluted basis. On closing of the second tranche, Agnico Eagle is expected to own about 69.1 million common shares, representing 13.5% of the Foran shares.

Agnico Eagle and Foran in August 2024 struck an agreement in which the former is entitled to certain rights, provided it maintains certain ownership thresholds. On the closing of the first tranche of the private placement, the agreement between the two companies will be amended to allow Agnico Eagle to participate in equity financings to maintain its pro rata ownership interest in Foran or acquire up to a 19.99% stake in Foran; and allow Agnico Eagle to nominate an additional individual to Foran's board if the board's size is increased to 10 or more directors.

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • NONE

Date of disclosure:

15/05/2025

Contact name:

Rosie Kinsella

Telephone number:

+44 (0)207 886 2574

The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.
